records.ety: [F. capable, LL. capabilis capacious, capable, fr. L. caper to take, contain. See Heave.]

defs: Possessing ability, qualification, or susceptibility; having capacity; of sufficient size or strength; as, a room capable of holding a large number; a castle capable of resisting a long assault.
quotes: Concious of joy and capable of pain.

defs: Possessing adequate power; qualified; able; fully competent; as, a capable instructor; a capable judge; a mind capable of nice investigations.
quotes: More capable to discourse of battles than to give them.

defs: Possessing legal power or capacity; as, a man capable of making a contract, or a will.

notes: ☞ Capable is usually followed by of, sometimes by an infinitive.
